summary: Prove the memory kind's File shape end-to-end now the gate dispatches memory members: a frontmatterless CLAUDE.md member imports and re-emits idempotently (insta snapshot)

per.section: A built-in kind is an adapter — two faces

new.description: The round-trip proof the shipped tests/memory_gate.rs does NOT carry: a `unit_shape = "file"` frontmatterless `claude-code.memory` member (a repo-root CLAUDE.md) imports and re-emits idempotently via an insta snapshot — the adapter read/emit face over a File-shaped unit with no YAML frontmatter (round-trip discipline, .claude/rules/rust.md; adapter_fidelity.rs only covers a Directory-shaped fieldless rule). Plus a resolution assertion that the two bare-`memory` providers (`claude-code.memory` + `agents-md.memory`) resolve by qualified identity — a lookup that does not collide resolves cleanly, and a bare `memory` reference is the AmbiguousKind load error. Do NOT re-test max_lines fire/silent — memory_gate.rs already proves it through `check --harness`.

tests.asserts: a no-frontmatter File-shaped memory member (CLAUDE.md) imports and re-emits byte-idempotently (insta snapshot); the two bare-`memory` providers resolve by qualified identity with no spurious collision on a non-colliding lookup, and a bare `memory` reference is AmbiguousKind.

acceptance: The gate already dispatches memory members (CHECK-MEMBERS-ALL-KINDS shipped) and the four curated files are on disk; this adds the missing File-shaped round-trip: `temper import` projects the frontmatterless CLAUDE.md member idempotently (insta snapshot), and the two-provider qualified resolution holds. cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: CHECK-MEMBERS-ALL-KINDS shipped (fb1dad3/e92bf62). tests/memory_gate.rs already proves max_lines fires/stays-silent via `check --harness`, un-double-reported by agents-md.memory. The gap: no test proves a `unit_shape=file` frontmatterless CLAUDE.md member imports idempotently (adapter_fidelity covers only a Directory-shaped fieldless rule). Don't duplicate memory_gate. Moving CLAUDE.md onto `.temper/` (the flip) is a human follow-on — fenced.

summary: Collapse the install self-verify to ONE summary advisory ('gate not installed — run temper install', with the counts) instead of one warn per artifact drowning the health signal

per.section: Decision: `install` projects the gate's wiring; drift keeps it synced

edit.description: `gate_installed` (install.rs:163) today maps each not-Unchanged placement to its own warn `Diagnostic`, so an uninstalled harness with 20 modelined artifacts emits ~22 install advisories that bury the real findings. Collapse to ONE summary advisory when any placement is missing/drifted — 'temper's gate is not installed or has drifted — run `temper install`' carrying the counts (hook, CI, N modelines) — the per-placement detail folded into the message body as a count/list, not N sibling diagnostics. Emit no advisory when every placement is Unchanged (unchanged behavior). Stays warn severity (session-start ignores warn).

Re-pin the self-verify assertions (~L249-283) that count one advisory per placement: the before-install case (every placement missing) now yields ONE summary advisory naming the counts, still advisory (never error); the drifted-single-placement case yields ONE summary advisory (not len()==1 keyed on the drifted placement); the fully-installed case stays empty. Keep the idempotence/dry-run install::run tests untouched — this narrows only gate_installed.

tests.asserts: an uninstalled or drifted harness yields exactly one summary install advisory carrying the missing/drifted counts, still warn-severity; a fully-installed harness yields none.

acceptance: On an unconfigured harness `check`/session-start emits a single install advisory (with the missing-placement counts) rather than one per artifact, so artifact findings own the output; a fully-installed harness emits none; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: gate_installed (src/install.rs:163) emits one warn PER drifted placement — ~20 modelines on a real target bury real findings. Collapse to a single advisory with the counts (hook/CI/N modelines); keep per-placement detail as a count/list behind it, not siblings. Stays warn (session-start ignores warn, so tests/session_start.rs's clean null-additionalContext holds). tests/install.rs asserts per-placement len() (~L254-283) — re-pin it. Disjoint from WEDGE-COVERAGE-NOTE (main.rs/lib.rs).

summary: Emit an advisory coverage note — which kinds checked N members, plus known-but-unmodeled Claude Code surfaces present on disk — so wedge silence never reads as 'checked'

new.description: A new module computing the wedge's coverage note: per-kind member counts (which built-in kinds checked how many members) plus a curated list of known Claude Code harness surfaces present on disk but governed by NO kind (e.g. `.claude/agents/`, `.claude/hooks/`, `.claude/settings.json`, a `specs/` corpus). Returns advisory (warn) `Diagnostic`s only. The known-surface list is an external fact — each entry cites the Claude Code docs at the point of claim (.claude/rules/collaboration.md, 'External facts are cited'). Scope honestly: only surfaces temper can cheaply detect on disk; no guessing, and no surface a built-in kind already governs (memory now governs CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md).

Fixture-driven: a harness with N skills + an unmodeled `.claude/agents/` dir yields a coverage note naming the checked kinds' counts and flagging the ungoverned agents surface; a harness whose surfaces are all modeled flags no unmodeled surface. All advisory (warn) — never error, never a session-start verdict.

edit.description: Add `pub mod coverage_note;` beside the existing module declarations (lib.rs:18-38).

In `check_gate` (~main.rs:840-852, beside the install self-verify), extend `diagnostics` with the coverage note over the harness root and the embedded built-in kind set (`builtin_kind::definitions()`), reusing the per-kind member counts the gate already loads. Advisory only, so the run's exit code and the session-start verdict are unchanged. No import.rs edit — the unmodeled-surface detection lives in the new module.

tests.asserts: the coverage note reports each kind's checked-member count and names known Claude Code surfaces present on disk that no kind governs; it is warn-severity, so it never fails the run or injects a session-start verdict.

acceptance: `temper check` on a harness carrying ungoverned surfaces (an agents/ dir, hooks, settings.json, a specs corpus) emits an advisory coverage note naming the checked kinds' counts and the unmodeled surfaces, so silence no longer reads as 'checked'; the note never gates; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Silence about surfaces temper has no kind for (agents/, hooks, settings.json, a specs/ corpus) reads as 'checked' — the fail-loud gap the inbox flags. Add an advisory note: per-kind member counts + a curated list of known Claude-Code surfaces present on disk but governed by no kind. That list is an external fact — cite the docs at the point of claim. Warn only (session-start ignores it). New module; no import.rs edit — disjoint from WEDGE-INSTALL-SUMMARY (install.rs).

summary: Re-pin every test/fixture using `version` as the example declared skill field onto `license` (an in-standard field), so the uncited version extraction can be dropped without reddening the tree

per.section: External facts are cited, never guessed

edit.description: Re-pin the `version` example declared-skill-field usage (~import.rs:914) onto `license` — a field documented in the agentskills.io standard — so the illustration stops leaning on an uncited field.
Re-pin the `version` example field in the frontmatter tests/doc examples (~frontmatter.rs:606-612) onto `license`.
Re-pin the `version` example declared field (~engine.rs:529) onto `license`.

Re-pin the extract_equivalence coordinate fixture (tests/fixtures/extract_equivalence/.claude/skills/coordinate/SKILL.md) and its regenerated snapshot (tests/snapshots/extract_equivalence__skill_features_coordinate.snap) from `version` onto `license`, so skill_features illustrates a standard field.

tests.asserts: the coordinate skill fixture declares `license` (not `version`) and its snapshot shows `license` among the extracted features; cargo test stays green with the version example fully re-pinned.

acceptance: No test or fixture leans on `version` as the example declared skill field; every site is re-pinned onto `license`; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green — so a human can drop the uncited `version` extraction line from kinds/claude-code/skill/KIND.md (fenced) without reddening the tree.
notes: `version` is in neither agentskills.io nor Claude Code's frontmatter table (re-fetched 2026-07-02); skill.anthropic's prose says metadata is the versioning home — uncited (collaboration: external facts cited). Re-pin import.rs, frontmatter.rs, engine.rs, tests/apply.rs, tests/readd.rs + the extract_equivalence fixture/snapshot onto `license`. KIND.md drop stays human (fenced). check.rs field("version") is a raw Member read — safe/excluded; grep for other version-as-declared sites.

summary: Extend the extraction algebra with the two primitives 15-kinds lists but code lacks — a `fenced` block extractor and nested key-path `field` lookup — held until a consumer kind needs them

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

gate.reason: no consumer — the extraction vocabulary is harvested from the built-in kinds and extended deliberately (15-kinds.md Decision: extraction is a closed algebra); no current kind (skill/rule/memory) reads a fenced block or a nested key-path, so adding either now is speculative scope with no kind to exercise it. Revive when a kind demands it — a JSON-manifest kind's nested key-path field, or a spec/class kind's fenced-block extraction. (NB memory's `@path` is a separate edge primitive, not one of these.)

edit.description: Two closed-vocabulary additions to `Primitive` (`specs/architecture/15-kinds.md`): (1) a `Fenced` primitive extracting fenced code-block content — the markdown-structure extractor the spec lists (`a fenced block`) but the enum omits (today: field/headings/sections/line_count/placement, confirmed by kinds/claude-code/memory/KIND.md); (2) make `Primitive::Field` honor the key-path its own doc-comment promises — the impl currently does a flat `unit.frontmatter.get(key)` so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read. Both extend the closed-vocab load-guard's accepted primitive set (parse_primitive). This entry owns both edits so they don't collide in parallel worktrees.

The extractor helpers behind the two primitives: a `body_fenced_blocks`-style reader over the markdown body (mirroring `body_headings`/`body_sections`), and key-path traversal for the field extractor (walk nested TOML/JSON tables to the leaf before `json_to_feature`, kind-preserving per the `type` lattice, `specs/architecture/10-contracts.md`).

tests.asserts: a `fenced` primitive yields the fenced block's content and skips prose; a key-path `field` reads a nested value and is absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve; both preserve the source scalar kind.

acceptance: `Primitive::Fenced` and a nested-key-path `Primitive::Field` are in the closed vocabulary, load-guarded, and extract over a `Unit`; a KIND.md declaring either reads the intended feature; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green. (Deferred: not built until a kind consumes one.)
notes: Disk re-confirmed both gaps: `Primitive` = field/headings/sections/line_count/placement (the memory KIND.md uses four of them); `Field` reads flat `frontmatter.get(key)`, no key-path; load-guard is parse_primitive. Neither breaks anything today (no kind uses them). ONE entry — both touch src/kind.rs + src/extract.rs. MEMORY-KIND does NOT revive this: `@path` is a distinct edge primitive, not fenced/key-path.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` as a member document, read through the generic loader and checked by the embedded `agent.anthropic` package

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; and post-adapter-wave the agent kind is mostly curated data (a KIND.md + PACKAGE.md, both outside build's fence) with near-zero engine code — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The embedded built-in `agent` KIND.md (curated — humans author it; outside build's fence), under its provider beside kinds/claude-code/skill and kinds/claude-code/rule with a `provider = "claude-code"` line: `governs = { root = '.claude/agents', glob = '*.md' }`, the declared `yaml-frontmatter` projection format + unit shape, composed extraction over the `name`/`description`/optional `tools`/`model` fields plus markdown structure. build.rs auto-embeds it — no engine extractor.

The agent kind's built-in package `agent.anthropic` (curated product territory, outside build's fence), embedded with the std-lib by build.rs. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`. Per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code subagent docs.

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence — all through the generic adapter + loader, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add `"agent"` to the `BUILTIN_KINDS` name-list so a `[kind.agent]` binding reads as a built-in layer, not a custom kind.
Add the `AGENT_PACKAGE = "agent.anthropic"` const beside SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE and register its kind->package binding. build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md; no manual embed edit.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the `agent.anthropic` package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently via the generic frontmatter adapter; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Declared-adapter architecture: no src/agent.rs IR — the generic adapter projects it. Curated kinds/claude-code/agent/KIND.md + packages/agent.anthropic/PACKAGE.md sit outside build's fence; engine wiring is near-zero (add "agent" to BUILTIN_KINDS + an AGENT_PACKAGE const/binding in builtin.rs; build.rs auto-embeds). Stays deferred: wrong direction under requirements/satisfies. NB shares src/kind.rs with EXTRACTION-VOCAB-GAPS and src/builtin.rs — serialize if un-deferred while either is open.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/architecture/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.

summary: Add CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md — the two missing launch community docs (two-sided AI-authorship disclosure; vuln-reporting evidence bar)

per.section: The community surface — small, honest, gated

gate.reason: build's writable fence (.flume/chain.ts BUILD_WRITABLE_PATHS) lists only README/CHANGELOG/AGENTS/LICENSE among root docs — no root CONTRIBUTING.md/SECURITY.md glob — so build would bail; a human must widen the fence (chain.ts is human territory) and enable private vulnerability reporting (a repo setting) before build can author these

new.description: Small and honest per the Decision: how to file a good issue, 'search first', PRs by prior discussion, and the two-sided AI-authorship policy (inbound AI-assisted contributions welcome WITH disclosure; the contributor must understand and defend the change without the assistant). Expectation-setting, not an invitation to co-maintain.

Vulnerability-reporting policy with an explicit evidence bar — a report must demonstrate, not speculate (the 'unverified AI-generated vuln reports are the new spam' lesson). Points at private vulnerability reporting (the GitHub repo setting a human enables).

acceptance: CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md exist at root, matching specs/intent/55-offering.md's launch-set Decisions (two-sided disclosure; demonstrate-not-speculate); the fence has been widened so build's entry-fence preflight admits them.
notes: Launch set (specs/intent/55-offering.md). Already on disk: README, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE-MIT/APACHE, issue forms (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug,feature,config}.yml). Missing only CONTRIBUTING.md + SECURITY.md. Both root-level per the spec but outside build's root-docs fence — parked on a human fence-widen (chain.ts) + enabling private reporting. Parked entries are skipped by the entry-fence gate, so this files cleanly.
